x-enumeration-literals.name: ACH Clearing
x-enumeration-literals.description:
ACH (Automated Clearing House) clearing: Batch clearing of payments, typically processed in cycles, suitable for low-value or recurring transactions.

Comment:
Examples:
SEPA credit transfers, US ACH payroll payments.

x-enumeration-literals.name: RTGS Clearing
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Real-Time Gross Settlement (RTGS) clearing: where each payment is processed individually and settled immediately. Used for high-value or time-critical payments.

Comment:
Examples
Interbank SWIFT payments, TARGET2 payments in the EU.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Card Network Clearing
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Clearing through card networks or schemes for card-based transactions, including debit and credit cards.

Comment:
Examples:
Visa, Mastercard, American Express card transactions.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Internal Clearing
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Transactions cleared within the same bank (on-us), without involving external clearing systems.

Comment:
Examples:
Transferring funds between two accounts in the same bank.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Correspondent Clearing
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Clearing through intermediary or correspondent banks, often for cross-border or foreign currency payments.    

Comment:
Examples:
USD payment from a Belgian bank to a US bank via a correspondent bank.
